title: Strobus koraiensis
taxonomy: Strobus koraiensis is classified as a species of coniferous tree in the genus Strobus, which is also known as the fir tree. It is a member of the family Pinaceae, commonly referred to as the pine family. Within the Pinaceae family, Strobus koraiensis is further classified as a type of evergreen tree. This classification is based on its needle-like leaves and conical shape, which are characteristic features of the genus Strobus. Strobus koraiensis is native to the Korean Peninsula and is considered an endangered species due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.

Pinus koraiensis, also known as Korean red pine, is a deciduous coniferous tree species native to East Asia. The health benefits of Pinus koraiensis include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Pinus koraiensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Pinus koraiensis is commonly used in traditional medicine and is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is also used as a food ingredient, particularly in the form of pine nuts, which are rich in healthy fats and antioxidants.
